mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 50 percent of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales and housing starts both exceed their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during periods of high inventory accumulation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of months where CPI inflation exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment remains stagnant above 7 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates above 2 percent annually fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of falling industrial production are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of historical cases.
